description: AC-697 slice 1 — canonical autoctx command contract. Single source of truth for Python and TypeScript parity. Each runtime's loader walks its CLI surface and a parity test verifies that every command claiming runtime_support.<runtime> == "yes" is actually registered at the canonical path. Follow-up AC-697 slices fill in friction fixes (status semantics, queue add parity, --iterations alias plumbing) and expand contract coverage to internal commands.

commands.summary: Take a natural-language objective, generate or select a scenario, run iterations against a real evaluator, and export the learned approach.

flags.name: iterations
flags.description: Number of refinement rounds. Canonical name across both runtimes. Legacy aliases (--gens, --rounds, --max-iterations) are tracked in AC-697 follow-up slices that add alias plumbing; the contract documents only honored options today.

flags.name: description
flags.description: Plain-language goal. The positional form (`autoctx solve "goal"`) is canonical shorthand.

commands.summary: Execute a saved scenario for one or more iterations and produce a Run artifact.

flags.name: iterations
flags.description: Number of generations to execute. Canonical name across both runtimes. Legacy aliases (--gens, --rounds) are tracked in AC-697 follow-up slices.

flags.name: run-id
flags.description: Operator-chosen run identifier. Generated if omitted.

commands.summary: Show run status for the given <run-id>. Canonical meaning is RUN status; queue status lives under `queue status`.
Stream live updates for an in-flight run.

python.reason: Python ships `autoctx wait <condition_id>` but not `watch <run-id>`. AC-697 follow-up slice adds a watch command that wraps the existing event-stream WebSocket.

commands.summary: Render a human-readable view of a finished run's artifacts (best strategy, scores, playbook).

python.reason: Python ships `replay` and `status` but no `show`. AC-697 follow-up slice adds `show` (with `--best`) that composes existing read surfaces.

flags.name: best
flags.description: Show only the best-scoring generation. Demo / agent-friendly.

commands.summary: Export a Run's learned knowledge as a portable artifact (JSON package, Pi-package directory, or Hermes skill).
Replay a stored generation from a run.
Score a single agent output against a rubric.
Run an evaluate-revise improvement loop against a rubric.
Manage the task queue. Subcommands cover add / status / list.

typescript.reason: TypeScript currently exposes `autoctx queue -s <spec> ...` for queue-add. AC-697 follow-up slice adds `autoctx queue add ...` and moves queue-add to the canonical path.

commands.summary: Show queue pending / in-flight counts. Distinct from top-level `status` (which is run status).
Materialize a new scenario from a description, template, or harness spec.
Long-running verified-goal mission manager (create / run / list).

python.reason: Mission is TypeScript-only today (per autocontext/README.md). Python parity is out of scope for AC-697 slice 1.

commands.summary: Start the HTTP API + WebSocket server.
Start the MCP server on stdio. Canonical path is `serve mcp`; legacy single-token `mcp-serve` is kept as a registered alias on both runtimes.

python.reason: Python ships at the legacy single-token path `mcp-serve` today. AC-697 follow-up slice registers the canonical `serve mcp` path and keeps `mcp-serve` as an alias.

typescript.reason: TypeScript ships at the legacy single-token path `mcp-serve` today. AC-697 follow-up slice mirrors the canonical `serve mcp` path.

commands.summary: Enumerate canonical commands, aliases, and per-runtime support. Operator-facing surface for the AC-697 contract.
